qa_pairs.question: How do you execute a command within an Apptainer container using the `apptainer run` command?
qa_pairs.answer: To execute a command within an Apptainer container, you use the `apptainer run` command. The basic structure is `apptainer run your-container-name.sif your-command`, where `your-container-name.sif` is the name of your Apptainer SIF file and `your-command` is the command you want to run inside the container.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide an example of how to compile and run a C++ program inside an Apptainer container?
qa_pairs.answer: To compile and then run a C++ program, for example, `myprog.cpp`, inside an Apptainer container, you would use two separate `apptainer run` commands. First, to compile the program: `apptainer run your-container-name.sif g++ -O2 -march=broadwell ./myprog.cpp`. After successful compilation, to run the resulting executable (named `a.out` by default): `apptainer run your-container-name.sif ./a.out`.
